name: Infoblox NIOS & BloxOne DDI API
what_it_does: Infoblox NIOS (on-premises) and BloxOne (cloud-native) REST API for DNS, DHCP, and IPAM (DDI) management platform. Enables AI agents to manage IP address space allocation and tracking, handle DNS zone and record management automation, access DHCP lease and scope management, retrieve DNS security threat intelligence and RPZ (Response Policy Zone) data, manage BloxOne Threat Defense DNS firewall, handle network discovery and device tracking, access IPAM reporting and capacity planning data, retrieve DNS query logging and analytics, manage NIOS appliance configuration and cluster management, and integrate DDI data with CMDB, ITSM, and network automation platforms.
use_cases: Automating IP address allocation from network provisioning agents
Managing DNS records from infrastructure automation agents
Retrieving DNS threat events from security operations agents
Integrating IPAM data with CMDB from network management agents
not_for: Public DNS resolution without enterprise DDI and IPAM management
Simple domain registration without enterprise IP address management
Consumer networking without enterprise DDI infrastructure
best_when: An enterprise using Infoblox NIOS or BloxOne wants AI agents to automate IP address management, DNS record lifecycle, DHCP scope management, DNS security threat processing, and CMDB/ITSM integration.
avoid_when: CRITICAL OPERATIONAL RISK: DNS record changes propagate globally and can cause service outages — always use TTL-aware changes and test DNS resolution before production deployment. DHCP scope changes can disrupt network connectivity for all devices in affected ranges. IP address deallocation without confirmation can cause address conflicts.

auth.notes: NIOS uses HTTP Basic auth and API keys. BloxOne Cloud uses API keys (Bearer token). Role-based access control with read/write permission scoping. infoblox-client Python library. Go SDK for BloxOne. Webhooks for DDI change events. Terraform provider for infrastructure-as-code. WAPI (Web API) version numbering for NIOS compatibility.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — hardware appliance or virtual appliance + annual support
pricing.paid_tiers: Perpetual hardware + annual support; BloxOne SaaS subscription per user/device
pricing.notes: Santa Clara, California. Founded 1999. Private (Vista Equity Partners). DDI market leader. 13,000+ customers. NIOS on-premises and BloxOne cloud-native platforms. DNS security via BloxOne Threat Defense. Strong government, financial services, and healthcare verticals. Competes with EfficientIP and BlueCat for enterprise DDI.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; NIOS has configurable API rate limits; BloxOne has documented SaaS limits

known_agent_gotchas: CRITICAL OPERATIONAL RISK: DNS record changes and DHCP scope modifications affect live network services — always use low TTL and staged rollout for DNS changes
NIOS WAPI versioning — NIOS API uses WAPI version in URL path; test against target NIOS version before production deployment
BloxOne vs NIOS API — different API endpoints and auth for cloud vs on-premises; verify target platform
DNS propagation delay — DNS changes are eventually consistent; implement TTL-aware automation with propagation verification
IPAM hierarchy — Networks, ranges, and hosts have complex parent-child relationships; understand hierarchy before automating allocation
Terraform provider available — infoblox/terraform-provider-infoblox for declarative IPAM and DNS management
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise DDI. SOC2, FedRAMP, FIPS-140. API key. On-premises/cloud. IP address, DNS, and DHCP infrastructure data.

security_score_components.security_notes: DDI management. SOC2, FedRAMP, FIPS-140. API key. On-premises/cloud. Network infrastructure and IP address data.
